ground_truth.title: What Are the Top U.S. Rental Car Companies of 2025?
ground_truth.author: Aoife O'Riordan
ground_truth.main_content: American analytics and consumer intelligence company J.D. Power recently released its 2025 report on top U.S. rental car companies of 2025. According to the J.D. Power 2025 North America Rental Car Satisfaction Study, Enterprise Rent-A-Car topped the charts for 2025, receiving a score of 734 out of 1,000. National came in second (721), and Advantage Rent A Car and Sixt tied for third (711). Hertz rounded out the top five. Last year, National Car Rental received first place. The lowest-rated rental car companies were Dollar, Fox Rent A Car and Payless. The report was compiled by examining customer experience with rental car companies across seven core dimensions in order of importance: ease of rental; pick-up/drop-off; level of trust; vehicle; staff; value for price paid; and digital tools. The report gathered responses from 8,263 business and leisure travelers who rented a vehicle at an airport location in the last year (August 2024–August 2025). Satisfaction was measured based on how long customers spend at an airport car rental counter. The report found travelers wait an average of eight minutes at the rental car counter. Eighty percent of airport rental car customers visit the counter first, despite many brands offering alternatives to skip the counter entirely. Overall satisfaction among customers who bypass the rental counter is 704, compared to 662 among those who stop at the counter.

According to the J.D. Power 2025 North America Rental Car Satisfaction Study, Enterprise Rent-A-Car topped the charts for 2025, receiving a score of 734 out of 1,000.
National came in second (721), and Advantage Rent A Car and Sixt tied for third (711). Hertz rounded out the top five.

The report was compiled by examining customer experience with rental car companies across seven core dimensions in order of importance: ease of rental; pick-up/drop-off; level of trust; vehicle; staff; value for price paid; and digital tools.
The report found travelers wait an average of eight minutes at the rental car counter.
